title: poa nemoralis f. coarctata
intro: Poa nemoralis f. coarctata, also known as the Wood Meadow Grass, is a subspecies of the wood meadow-grass plant, which is a perennial grass native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Poa nemoralis f. coarctata include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting digestive health, making it a valued remedy in traditional herbalism. Herbal preparations of Poa nemoralis f. coarctata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
